pr_description:
## Overview
This PR continues the `views/redux/info` TypeScript migration and updates reducer/test patterns to align with Redux Toolkit action creators.

## Key Changes
- Migrated reducers to RTK `createSlice` + `extraReducers(addCase(...))` where possible:
  - `views/redux/info/fleets.ts`
  - `views/redux/info/useitems.ts`
  - `views/redux/info/resources.ts` (now uses middleware for cross-slice cases)
  - `views/redux/info/equips.ts` (now uses middleware for cross-slice cases)
- Updated info reducer tests to dispatch the real action creators from `views/redux/actions.ts` (required for `addCase` matching) and refreshed snapshots.
- Added small internal actions in `views/redux/actions.ts` to support middleware-derived updates:
  - `@@info.resources@ApplyDelta`
  - `@@info.equips@RemoveByIds`

## Cross-Slice Workarounds (Why Middleware)
RTK slice reducers don鈥檛 get access to root state, but some updates must be derived from other slices.

To keep reducers pure:
- `views/redux/middlewares/resources-cross-slice.ts` reads `store.getState()`, computes resource deltas, then dispatches `@@info.resources@ApplyDelta`.
- `views/redux/middlewares/equips-cross-slice.ts` reads `store.getState()`, derives equip IDs from ship slots, then dispatches `@@info.equips@RemoveByIds`.

Resources cases handled by middleware:
- `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_kousyou/createship_speedchange`: infer LSC from `info.constructions[kdockId - 1].api_item1` to subtract 10 vs 1 instant-build materials.
- `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_nyukyo/start`: subtract repair fuel/steel (and optionally a bucket) from `info.ships[shipId].api_ndock_item`.

Equips cases handled by middleware:
- `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_kaisou/powerup`: when `api_slot_dest_flag != 0`, remove equips from sacrificed ships.
- `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_kousyou/destroyship`: when `api_slot_dest_flag != 0`, remove equips from scrapped ships.

## kcsapi Missing Types (Explicit Callouts)
Some API endpoints used by this repo are not typed in the `kcsapi` package. We define local request/response interfaces in `views/redux/actions.ts` with `FIXME` callouts:
- `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_hensei/preset_order_change`
- `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_member/updatedeckname`
- `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_air_corps/change_name`
- `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_air_corps/change_deployment_base`

## Remaining Cross-Slice Reads Not Handled In This PR
This PR only addresses cross-slice reads that were blocking the RTK migrations above (resources + equips). Other legacy reducers still rely on the custom reducer signature (`reducer(state, action, store?)`) or globals:
- `views/redux/info/ships.ts` reads `store` for `@@Response/kcsapi/api_req_nyukyo/speedchange`.
- `views/redux/info/quests.ts` reads `store` in quest tracking logic, and uses `window.getStore(...)` in `saveQuestTracking()`.

## Tests
- `npm test -- --testPathPattern=\"views/redux/(info|middlewares)/__tests__\"`

## Follow-ups
- TODO(rtk-migration): `views/redux/info/quests.ts` still uses the legacy reducer signature `reducer(state, action, store)` and reads cross-slice/root state via the `store` argument (ships/fleets/equips/const/sortie/battle) to infer quest progress; RTK slice reducers only receive `(state, action)`, so this logic needs to move to middleware/listeners (or actions must carry the required context) before we can migrate quests to `createSlice` + `extraReducers(addCase(...))`.
- TODO(persistence): `saveQuestTracking()` persists via a store observer + `window.getStore()`. In a follow-up, move this to an explicit persistence middleware/listener for clearer side-effect boundaries and better testability.
commit_message: Use response-saver fixtures in info tests
